name: Translation Initiation
description: Eukaryotic translation initiation: eIF4F binds the 5' cap, the 43S pre-initiation complex scans the 5' UTR for an AUG start codon, recognition triggers eIF2 GTP hydrolysis, and 60S joining forms an elongation-competent 80S ribosome.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["mRNA with 5' cap"]
    N2["eIF4F binds cap"]
    N3["43S complex recruited"]
    N4["Scan 5' UTR"]
    N5{"AUG start codon?"}
    N6["Continue scanning"]
    N7["eIF2 GTP hydrolysis"]
    N8["60S joins: 80S complex"]
    N9["Elongation-ready ribosome"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 -->|no| N6
    N6 -->|scan| N4
    N5 -->|yes| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

nodeDetails.label: Scan 5' UTR
nodeDetails.detail: Ribosome scans toward the start codon

sources.title: The mechanism of eukaryotic translation initiation and principles of its regulation
sources.authors: Jackson, R. J.; Hellen, C. U.; Pestova, T. V.

sources.title: Regulation of translation initiation in eukaryotes: mechanisms and biological targets
The scanning mechanism of eukaryotic translation initiation
